title: Patent Ductus Arteriosus in the Preterm Infant

blocks.text:
ทำไม neonatologist ต้องเข้าใจ PDA ใน Preterm?
PDA พบใน 96% ของ GA 24-25 wk ที่ day 4 (vs 50% in full-term ที่ปิดภายใน 24 hr). Mechanism ที่ prevent preterm DA closure: ↑PGE2 sensitivity (↑EP4 coupling + ↓PDE) + ↓Kv channels (oxygen-sensitive) + immature smooth muscle isoforms. Hemodynamic effects: diastolic run-off → ↓systemic BP, ↓mesenteric/renal blood flow → NEC/AKI; pulmonary overcirculation → ↑BPD risk. Treatment controversy: indomethacin vs ibuprofen vs acetaminophen vs conservative; PDA-TOLERATE trial (2019) shows no clear benefit of early routine treatment on BPD/death. Long-term: moderate-large PDA + intubation ≥10 days → ↑BPD/death; ≤10 days intubation → no difference.
เป้าหมายการเรียนรู้วันนี้
อธิบาย factors ที่ promote ductal constriction (O2, endothelin, Rho kinase) vs patency (PGE2, NO, intraluminal pressure)
รู้ developmental differences: preterm vs term DA (↑PGE2 sensitivity, ↓Kv channels, ↓Rho kinase)
รู้ incidence ตาม GA: ตาราง PDA any size vs hemodynamically significant PDA by day
รู้ echocardiographic criteria: diameter ≥1.5 mm, LA/Ao ≥1.5, diastolic reversal aorta, LPA end-diastolic velocity ≥0.20 m/s
อธิบาย hemodynamic effects: systemic steal, pulmonary overcirculation, organ blood flow redistribution
รู้ pharmacology: indomethacin (mechanisms, PK, side effects), ibuprofen (vs indo), acetaminophen (PGH2 synthetase inhibition)
รู้ surgical ligation complications: vocal cord paralysis 20-60%, post-ligation cardiac syndrome 30-50%
รู้ transcatheter PDA device closure (Piccolo device, weight ≥700g)
รู้ prophylactic indomethacin: ↓IVH (especially males), no effect on BPD/NEC long-term
รู้ current treatment controversy: to treat or not; who benefits most
1. Diagnosis of PDA
1.1 Clinical Signs
Systolic murmur, hyperdynamic precordium, full pulses, widened pulse pressure, worsening respiratory status
Clinical signs appear LATER than echocardiographic signs
Tachycardia: NOT reliable indicator of PDA in preterm
Large left-to-right shunt: cardiomegaly + ↑pulmonary arterial markings on CXR
1.2 Echocardiographic Criteria

rows: Poor sensitivity/specificity for shunt magnitude; CANNOT replace echo

blocks.text: 1.3 PDA Severity Classification
Small PDA: hemodynamics similar to no PDA; same outcomes
Moderate PDA: moderate left-to-right shunt; associated with significant neonatal morbidities
Large PDA: severe shunting; ↑pulmonary overcirculation + ↓systemic perfusion
Hemodynamically significant PDA (hsPDA): diameter ≥2 mm + need for ventilator support
2. Incidence ตาม Gestational Age
Essentially all preterm ≥30 wk close by day 4. Among ≤27 wk who close: 80% reopen if ANY residual Doppler flow after indomethacin
Spontaneous closure by 1 yr: 86% of infants discharged with PDA
3. Regulation of Ductal Patency
3.1 Vasoconstrictors (close ductus)
O2: depolarizes smooth muscle by inhibiting K+ channels (especially Kv1.5, Kv2.1) → Ca2+ influx through L-type and T-type channels → constriction
Mitochondria → O2 sensor: ↑PO2 → mitochondrial fission (DRP1) → ↑ROS (H2O2) → ↑Ca2+ influx + Rho kinase activation
Endothelin-1: potent constrictor via IP3 → Ca2+ from sarcoplasmic reticulum; role varies by species (negligible in human)
Rho kinase: ↑smooth muscle Ca2+ sensitivity by inhibiting myosin light chain phosphatase
Catecholamines, decrease in intraluminal pressure
3.2 Vasodilators (maintain patency)
PGE2: most important in utero; acts via EP2/EP3/EP4 receptors → ↑cAMP → ↓myosin light chain kinase → vasodilation; ductus extremely sensitive to PGE2 (low PDE → ↑cAMP accumulation)
Nitric oxide: sGC → ↑cGMP → PKG → ↓intracellular Ca2+; important in rodents in utero; less clear in human ductus
Intraluminal pressure (high PVR in utero → ↑luminal pressure → opposes constriction)
KATP channels, KCa channels (early gestation, not O2-regulated)
CO, H2S (physiologic role unclear)
3.3 ทำไม Preterm DA ไม่ปิด (Box 48.2)
Immature smooth muscle myosin isoforms (weaker contractile capacity)
↓Ca2+ entry via L-type Ca2+ channels (especially under hypoxia)
↓Rho kinase expression/activity → ↓Ca2+ sensitization
KCa channels (not O2-regulated) predominate over Kv channels (O2-regulated) in preterm
↑Circulating PGE2 (premature lung clears PGE2 less efficiently)
↑Sensitivity to PGE2: ↑EP4 coupling with adenylyl cyclase + ↓PDE → ↑cAMP per receptor stimulus
↓Endothelin production; Absence of vasa vasorum (no profound hypoxia → no anatomic remodeling)
3.4 Anatomic Closure
Requires: tight constriction → profound ischemic hypoxia → HIF1α, VEGF → smooth muscle apoptosis + neointimal mounds
VEGF: endothelial proliferation + vasa vasorum proliferation → permanent occlusion
Monocytes adhere to VCAM-1 → PDGF → smooth muscle migration into neointima → lumen occlusion
Preterm DA: thin wall → NO vasa vasorum (<26 wk) → luminal flow provides O2 to entire wall → minimal hypoxia unless COMPLETE cessation of flow → no anatomic remodeling
4. Hemodynamic and Pulmonary Effects
4.1 Systemic Effects
Diastolic run-off → ↓systemic BP (both systolic and diastolic) → ↑inotrope-dependent hypotension in <28 wk
Blood flow redistribution: ↓mesenteric (both fasting and fed), ↓renal blood flow → ↑NEC risk, ↓GFR
Cerebral O2 saturation compromised with moderate-large PDA (limited autoregulation in preterm); small-for-GA at even greater risk (↑impaired cerebrovascular autoregulation)
Evidence: any negative impact on brain growth may be more about cerebral O2 saturation than shunt magnitude or treatment (Lemmers 2016)
With intact cerebral autoregulation: cerebral O2 saturation may be preserved → can delay treatment while monitoring NIRS
4.2 Pulmonary Effects
PDA ↑incidence early hemorrhagic pulmonary edema/pulmonary hemorrhage (RCTs: Kluckow 2014, Al-Faleh 2008, Aranda 2009)
Surfactant → rapid ↓PVR → ↑L→R shunting → pulmonary hemorrhage risk
PDA → ↑hydraulic pressure in pulmonary vasculature (arterial + venous sides)
Compensatory: ↑lung lymph flow (edema safety factor) → first few days, no net lung water accumulation
After day 6-10: ↓pulmonary capillary surface area → ↑filtration → pulmonary edema → ↑compliance problems
PDA-TOLERATE RCT (preterm baboon): pharmacological closure → ↑alveolarization vs open PDA arrest in alveolar development (hallmark "new BPD")
BUT surgical ligation does NOT have same benefit (↑inflammation genes, ↓epithelial Na channels → ↓water clearance)
5. Treatment Options
5.1 Pharmacologic — COX Inhibitors

rows: 10 mg/kg, then 5 mg/kg q24h × 3 doses IV or oral
Less renal/mesenteric/cerebral vasoconstriction; ↑bilirubin displacement (caution in jaundice); no ICH sparing effect
15 mg/kg q6h × 5-7 days IV or oral
Mechanism: peroxidase moiety PGH2 synthetase inhibition (NOT COX); least renal effects; possible neurotoxicity concern (prenatal exposure)
Non-inferior in most RCTs; RCT <28 wk: indomethacin > acetaminophen > ibuprofen (Liebowitz J Perinatol 2019)

blocks.text: 5.2 Indomethacin Pharmacokinetics
Half-life: day 1 = 71 hr; day 7 = 21 hr; adjust dosing accordingly
Single loading dose (0.2 mg/kg) within 24 hr after delivery: effective for prophylaxis
Extended low-dose course (0.1 mg/kg q24h × 5-7 days): ↑permanent closure especially if residual Doppler flow after standard course
Prophylactic indomethacin (within 18 hr): ↓IVH (especially severe IVH in male preterm); neuroprotective effect via accelerating germinal matrix microvessel maturation and ↓CBF (NOT via PDA closure)
Contraindication: concurrent hydrocortisone (week 1) → ↑intestinal perforation
5.3 Surgical Ligation
Definitive closure but significant morbidities:
Unilateral vocal cord paralysis: 20-60% ELBW (↑tube feedings + respiratory support, persists beyond neonatal period)
Post-ligation cardiac syndrome: 30-50% of BW ≤1000g (impaired adrenal response, ↓cortisol, ↓vascular tone → hypotension)
↑BPD development with early ligation
↑Neurodevelopmental abnormalities after early ligation
Delaying/avoiding ligation: ↓morbidities (Wickremasinghe 2012, Chorne Pediatrics 2007)
Short-term respiratory improvement after ligation: ONLY in infants on HFV pre-ligation; those on conventional ventilation pre-ligation → no improvement + ↑support early post-op
5.4 Transcatheter PDA Closure (TCPC)
Piccolo device (Amplatzer): approved for TCPC in preterm; feasible for BW <1000g
↓ECMO exposure vs surgery; no RCT comparing TCPC vs pharmacologic/conservative
Major adverse events: 5-10%; death: ~2% (↑with smaller size + less experienced centers)
Growing use for preterm >700g; being explored in <700g
5.5 Conservative/Expectant Management
Fluid restriction: ↓PDA symptoms but NOT reduce shunt; ↓systemic blood flow
Diuretics: improve pulmonary edema/compliance; small effect on L→R shunt
Dopamine: ↓shunting by ↑PVR; note: also ↑pulmonary resistance
PEEP ↑: small effect on PDA shunt
PDA-TOLERATE Trial (Clyman J Pediatr 2019): early routine treatment (starting day 8) vs conservative in moderate-large PDA — NO difference BPD/NEC/mortality
But: both groups had prolonged PDA exposure → treatment failure → cannot conclude conservative is equivalent

KEY FINDING: PDA + BPD Association — 2 Criteria Must Be Met
BPD + PDA association exists ONLY when BOTH: (1) moderate-large shunt persists >7-14 days AND (2) mechanical ventilation required ≥10 days; infants intubated <10 days: same BPD rate regardless of PDA → routine PDA closure does NOT help BPD in infants who extubate quickly.
6. Prophylactic Indomethacin
6.1 Evidence for Prophylaxis
Schmidt B et al. NEJM. 2001;344:1966 (TIPP): N=1202, <1000g; prophylactic indo (within 6 hr) vs placebo; ↓IVH, ↓severe IVH, ↓symptomatic PDA, ↓surgical ligation; NO difference BPD, NEC, death
Vohr BR et al. Pediatrics. 2003;111:e340: TIPP school-age outcomes. Boys receiving prophylaxis: better cognitive scores at 4.5 and 8 yr
Ment LR et al. J Pediatr. 2004;145:832: Prophylactic indomethacin specifically benefits male preterm infants in reducing IVH — sex-specific benefit
Mechanism: NOT via PDA closure; ↓CBF → ↓germinal matrix reperfusion; ↑germinal matrix microvessel maturation
6.2 Current Practice
Prophylactic indo: used by some centers, especially for male preterm <28 wk
Less used now: overall IVH rates have declined (better care practices); limited RCT evidence for contemporary care
Indomethacin prophylaxis remains one of few interventions with evidence for ↓IVH in VLBW
7. NICU Practical — PDA Management
ทารก <28 wk: assess echo at 24-48 hr; ถ้า large L→R PDA → ประเมิน hemodynamic significance: BP, urine output, NEC signs, NIRS
Hemodynamically significant PDA (hsPDA): ductus diameter ≥2 mm + ventilator-dependent → พิจารณา treatment
Treatment decision matrix:
≤10 days intubation predicted: observe; ลด fluid; optimize ventilator
>10 days intubation + moderate-large PDA >7-14 days: strongly consider pharmacologic closure
Pulmonary hemorrhage: treat aggressively with indomethacin/ibuprofen
Prophylactic indo: male <28 wk, especially if high risk for IVH
ตรวจ NIRS (renal + cerebral rSO2) ร่วมกับ echo เพื่อ assess organ perfusion
Indomethacin: check renal function before/after; avoid concurrent hydrocortisone week 1; ตรวจ bilirubin ถ้าให้ ibuprofen (เพราะ displaces bilirubin from albumin)
Post-ligation: prepare for post-ligation cardiac syndrome (hypotension 30-50%) → have hydrocortisone ready, be cautious with dopamine (may need milrinone if LV afterload ↑)
8. 5 Bedside Pearls — สำหรับใช้พรุ่งนี้

PEARL 1 — PDA Echocardiography: LA/Ao Ratio + Diastolic Flow พร้อมกัน
LA/Ao ≥1.5 = significant left-to-right shunting; retrograde diastolic flow in descending aorta = systemic steal; end-diastolic LPA velocity ≥0.20 m/s = L→R flow; ไม่มี single parameter ที่ reliable 100% — combine ≥2 criteria; interobserver variability 20% for all echo measures.

PEARL 2 — Indomethacin Half-life Day 1 = 71 hr → ไม่ให้ชิดกัน
Half-life ลดตาม postnatal age: day 1 = 71 hr → ต้อง space dose adequately (q12-24 hr ไม่ใช่ q6-8 hr); day 7 = 21 hr → standard dosing; ถ้าให้ข้น: drug accumulates → ↑renal vasoconstriction → ↑AKI; สำคัญ: avoid concurrent hydrocortisone week 1 → ↑intestinal perforation risk; indomethacin → ↓CBF (protective for IVH, not harmful).

PEARL 3 — Surgical Ligation: ระวัง Vocal Cord Paralysis 20-60%
ทุก neonate ที่ PDA ligation ควรตรวจ vocal cord mobility post-operatively; vocal cord paralysis → ↑aspiration risk, ↑respiratory support need, ↑tube feeding requirement, persists beyond neonatal period; post-ligation cardiac syndrome (30-50% BW ≤1000g): sudden ↓BP 4-12 hr post-op → have hydrocortisone + milrinone ready.

PEARL 4 — Conservative PDA Management: ดู NIRS ไม่ใช่แค่ BP
ถ้าเลือก conservative management: monitor cerebral rSO2 + renal rSO2; cerebral rSO2 preserved with intact autoregulation → safe to watch; renal rSO2 ↓ → end-organ compromise → reconsider treatment; BP alone insufficient: wide pulse pressure (diastolic run-off) may not reflect cerebral perfusion adequacy.

PEARL 5 — BPD + PDA: 2 Criteria ต้องมีพร้อมกัน
PDA + BPD association มีเฉพาะเมื่อ: (1) moderate-large PDA persist >7-14 days + (2) intubation ≥10 days; ถ้า intubation <10 days → same BPD rate regardless of PDA; หมายความว่า routine PDA closure ไม่ช่วย BPD ในเด็กที่ extubate เร็ว; treat PDA aggressively ใน infants with severe lung disease ที่ต้องการ ventilation ยาวนาน.
9. Evidence Update — Landmark Studies
Gersony WM et al. J Pediatr. 1983;102:895 (NICHD Collaborative Study): First large RCT indomethacin vs surgery vs conservative for PDA; established indomethacin as effective. Seminal study.
Schmidt B et al. NEJM. 2001;344:1966 (TIPP): Prophylactic indo in ELBW. ↓IVH, ↓PDA, ↓ligation; NO improvement BPD/NEC/death. School-age: boys benefit cognitively (Vohr 2003, Ment 2004).
Van Overmeire B et al. NEJM. 2000;343:674: Ibuprofen vs indomethacin for PDA; equal closure rates; ibuprofen: less renal impairment, less oliguria, less NEC.

Ohlsson A, Walia R, Shah SS. Cochrane 2020 (CD003481): Ibuprofen vs indomethacin for treatment of PDA. Similar closure rates; ibuprofen: ↓NEC, ↓renal insufficiency; preferred over indomethacin for these outcomes.

Liebowitz M et al. J Perinatol. 2019;39:599: PDA-TOLERATE secondary analysis, <28 wk, day 7-10, moderate-large PDA: Indomethacin RR 3.21 vs spontaneous closure; ibuprofen RR 2.03; acetaminophen RR 1.33 (NS). Indomethacin most effective for constriction in most immature infants.

Clyman RI et al. J Pediatr. 2019;205:41 (PDA-TOLERATE trial): Early routine treatment (day 8) vs conservative, moderate-large PDA. NO difference BPD/NEC/mortality. Both groups prolonged PDA exposure — trial limitations acknowledged.
Sathanandam SK et al. Catheter Cardiovasc Interv. 2020;96:1266: Piccolo Occluder clinical trial for percutaneous closure ≥700g. Feasible, effective alternative to surgical ligation.
Katheria A et al. JAMA. 2019;322:1877: Cord milking ↑severe IVH/death vs delayed cord clamping in <32 wk. NOT recommended despite hemodynamic benefits.
Al-Faleh K et al. Pediatrics. 2008;121:e233: Prophylactic indomethacin prevents pulmonary hemorrhage in ELBW (18-month follow-up of TIPP).

Wickremasinghe AC et al. J Pediatr. 2012;161:1065: Early ligation vs selective ligation PDA. Delayed/selective ligation: ↓post-ligation hypotension, ↓vocal cord paralysis, ↓neurodevelopmental morbidity.
Lemmers PM et al. Pediatrics. 2016;137:e20153090: PDA and brain volume. Cerebral O2 saturation more associated with brain growth than PDA magnitude or treatment. Supports NIRS-guided management.

McCurnin D et al. Pediatrics. 2008;121:945: Ibuprofen-induced PDA closure in preterm baboons: ↑alveolarization vs open PDA (arrest in alveolar development). Surgical ligation did NOT show same lung benefit.
10. Self-Check Questions พร้อมเฉลย
Q: Echo criteria สำหรับ hemodynamically significant PDA?

ตอบ: (1) Ductus diameter ≥1.5 mm (or >50% LPA diameter); (2) LA/Ao ratio ≥1.5 (left heart volume overload); (3) Retrograde diastolic flow in descending aorta (systemic steal); (4) End-diastolic LPA velocity ≥0.20 m/s; หมายเหตุ: BNP/NTpBNP ↑ correlates with moderate PDA but poor sensitivity/specificity for shunt magnitude changes — cannot replace echo; interobserver variability 20% for all parameters.
Q: ทำไม preterm DA ไม่ปิดหลังคลอดเหมือน term?

ตอบ: (1) ↑PGE2 sensitivity: ↑EP4-adenylyl cyclase coupling + ↓PDE → ↑cAMP per PGE2 molecule; (2) ↓Kv channels (oxygen-sensitive) → ↓O2-induced depolarization; (3) ↑KCa channels (not O2-regulated) → maintained relaxation; (4) Immature smooth muscle myosin isoforms → ↓contractile capacity; (5) ↓Rho kinase → ↓Ca2+ sensitization; (6) ↑Circulating PGE2 (premature lung clears less efficiently); (7) Absent vasa vasorum (<26 wk) → thin wall → luminal flow provides O2 → no profound hypoxia → no anatomic remodeling.
Q: Indomethacin vs ibuprofen — เมื่อไหร่จะเลือกอะไร?

ตอบ: Indomethacin PREFERRED ถ้า: (1) High IVH risk (male, <28 wk) — IVH prophylaxis effect via ↓CBF + germinal matrix maturation (ibuprofen ไม่มี effect นี้); (2) Most immature infants <28 wk with moderate-large PDA (Liebowitz 2019: indomethacin most effective for constriction); Caution: concurrent hydrocortisone week 1 → avoid; Ibuprofen PREFERRED ถ้า: (1) Significant jaundice concern; (2) Renal compromise; (3) NO IVH prophylaxis needed; Acetaminophen ALTERNATIVE ถ้า: indomethacin/ibuprofen contraindicated; least renal effects; but less effective in <28 wk RCT.
Q: อธิบาย anatomic closure mechanism และทำไม preterm DA ไม่ undergo anatomic remodeling?

ตอบ: Term DA closure sequence: (1) Tight smooth muscle constriction; (2) → profound ischemic hypoxia of vessel wall (vasa vasorum obliterated); (3) → HIF1α, VEGF → smooth muscle apoptosis; (4) Monocytes adhere to VCAM-1 → PDGF → neointimal migration; (5) Lumen occluded permanently; Preterm DA failure: ductus wall very thin (<26 wk: no vasa vasorum); luminal blood flow provides O2 to entire wall even with partial constriction; requires COMPLETE cessation of luminal flow for ischemia; without adequate ischemia: vessel remains responsive to vasodilators → reopens; this explains why early (partial closure) indomethacin → 80% reopen if any residual Doppler flow.
Q: ผลของ PDA ต่อ organ blood flow ใน preterm?

ตอบ: (1) Diastolic run-off from aorta → ↓diastolic BP → ↓coronary diastolic perfusion; (2) Mesenteric: ↓blood flow + ↓rSO2 (both fasting + fed states) → NEC risk; (3) Renal: ↓blood flow + ↓GFR; (4) Cerebral: ↓oxygenation with moderate-large PDA + limited autoregulation → but if autoregulation intact: cerebral O2 preserved → safe to monitor while awaiting spontaneous closure (Lemmers 2016); (5) Pulmonary: ↑blood flow → ↑hydraulic pressure → interstitial edema; compensated by ↑lymph flow first days, then fails after day 6-10.
Q: Prophylactic indomethacin — evidence สนับสนุนและ current practice?

ตอบ: TIPP (Schmidt NEJM 2001): prophylactic indo (first 6 hr) in ELBW → ↓IVH, ↓symptomatic PDA, ↓ligation; NO improvement BPD/NEC/death; school-age: boys → better cognitive outcomes (Vohr 2003, Ment 2004); mechanism: NOT via PDA closure → via ↓CBF + ↑germinal matrix microvessel maturation; current practice: used by some centers esp. for high-IVH-risk male <28 wk; overall IVH rates have declined → some question routine use; prophylactic ibuprofen does NOT have same IVH-sparing effect.
Q: Conservative management criteria — when is it safe?

ตอบ: Current evidence: PDA + BPD association exists ONLY when BOTH conditions met: (1) moderate-large PDA persists >7-14 days + (2) intubation ≥10 days; if intubation <10 days: same BPD rate regardless of PDA → conservative safe; monitor NIRS: if cerebral rSO2 preserved (autoregulation intact) → can delay; if renal/cerebral rSO2 ↓ → treat; if pulmonary hemorrhage: treat aggressively; PDA-TOLERATE: early treatment (day 8) vs conservative → no BPD/NEC/mortality difference but both had prolonged PDA → study design limitation.
Q: Post-ligation cardiac syndrome — mechanism and management?

ตอบ: Incidence: 30-50% BW ≤1000g; timing: 4-12 hr post-op; mechanism: (1) Sudden ↑SVR (no more diastolic run-off → immature LV not prepared for acute afterload increase); (2) ↓Cortisol/adrenal response; (3) ↓Vascular tone; presentation: sudden systemic hypotension requiring inotropes; management: (1) Milrinone (↓afterload, ↑contractility) — may prevent by starting prophylactically pre-ligation in ELBW; (2) Hydrocortisone if vasopressor-resistant; (3) Dopamine for BP support; prevention: avoid early routine ligation (delay until older if possible).
Day 49 Preview

Avery Chapter 49 — Perinatal Arrhythmias (Chun & Arya)
พรุ่งนี้: Perinatal Arrhythmias (Avery Ch.49, Chun & Arya) — เปลี่ยนหัวข้อจาก vasculature มาสู่ electrical system; เราจะ cover: AV blocks (1st/2nd/3rd degree), SVT mechanisms (ORT vs AET vs PJRT vs atrial flutter), CCAVB (anti-SSA/Ro antibodies → dexamethasone treatment, pacemaker indications), และ fetal arrhythmia management. Key drugs: adenosine (acute SVT), propranolol (chronic), sotalol/flecainide/amiodarone (refractory). Clinical pearl: ทารกที่ดูเหมือน 'ซึมเล็กน้อย' อาจกำลัง develop incessant SVT → dilated cardiomyopathy — diagnose early!
